query: How does v-if affect component creation and destruction?

competitors.label: Conditional Rendering
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical Vue guide for v-if, v-show, mount versus hide behavior, and toggle-cost trade-offs.
theirStrengths: The official guide is authoritative on conditional rendering semantics and the higher-toggle-cost versus higher-initial-cost distinction.
theirGaps: It is lighter on the practical debugging fallout around local-state resets, cleanup, and hidden reactive work.

realWorldUseCases: it is a mount or unmount decision.
local state, timers, watchers, focus, and in-flight UI context can disappear each time the condition turns false.
followUpConfusion: v-if is structural: it creates and destroys DOM and component instances.

realWorldUseCases: The docs explain the core rendering trade-off well, but our page is more explicit about the exact state-reset and cleanup bugs developers see.
actionableExamples: The official examples are strong enough that the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our answer is clearer on the likely follow-up that v-if is not a cheap visibility toggle and that changing key can force recreation even when the branch stays visible.

actionableExamples: Add one tiny child-form example later showing draft reset under v-if versus preserved state under v-show.

competitors.winReason: The official guide is authoritative on the toggle trade-off, but our page is stronger because it translates that rule into concrete state-reset and lifecycle-cleanup behavior.

competitors.label: Vue Interview Questions
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd's Vue interview bank is directly relevant because conditional rendering, lifecycle, and state-preservation trade-offs are common Vue interview themes.
theirStrengths: It is a strong interview-prep hub for Vue rendering and lifecycle fundamentals.
theirGaps: The category page is broader than this exact mount-versus-hide decision.

realWorldUseCases: The common mistake is treating v-if like cheap show or hide behavior when it actually resets the branch.
followUpConfusion: Toggling it re-runs setup/data and resets local state.

realWorldUseCases: The bank is clearly relevant, but it is less specific on the exact preservation-versus-teardown trade-off of v-if versus v-show.
actionableExamples: GreatFrontEnd remains competitive on example-driven practice, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: The landing page is less explicit about lifecycle cleanup, local state resets, and when a key change deliberately forces recreation.

actionableExamples: Keep the v-if versus v-show comparison short and centered on mount/unmount versus CSS-only toggling.

competitors.winReason: GreatFrontEnd is useful as a Vue interview hub, but our page is stronger because it answers this exact lifecycle and state-persistence decision directly.

competitors.label: v-if vs. v-show - Vue Conditional Rendering
competitors.selectionReason: LearnVue has a topic-direct comparison article for the v-if versus v-show decision and its performance implications.
theirStrengths: It is a direct topic match and clearly explains DOM removal versus CSS display toggling.
theirGaps: It is lighter on local-state resets, watcher behavior, and forced recreation through keys.

realWorldUseCases: For components, it is a mount or unmount decision
The common mistake is treating v-if like cheap show or hide behavior
followUpConfusion: If you want to deliberately reset a component, you can change its key.

realWorldUseCases: The article already explains the core DOM and performance difference, but our page is more explicit about the component-instance and cleanup implications.
